prompt: Build a local-first fantasy football score tracker module for a small private league (8-12 managers). The module must: (1) Allow configuring league name and roster slots; (2) Enable weekly score entry per team roster (manual entry or paste from external source); (3) Calculate weekly team totals from roster slot scores; (4) Maintain running season totals and display simple standings; (5) Work entirely offline with local storage; (6) Support export of standings for sharing. Do NOT build draft tools, trade analyzers, or multi-league management. Keep it focused on weekly score tracking and season totals for one league.
- board type or custom grid (Chess, Checkers, Go, etc.) - players (local vs remote) - turn order and who starts - current state / last move
Generate 1-50 randomized bingo cards with NxN grid on local device, print-ready or digital display
The user is writing/editing content and encounters or recalls a term whose appropriateness they're unsure of
child playing a categorization game on tablet, selecting correct image from grid
team1Name, team2Name, team1Q1, team1Q2, team1Q3, team1Q4, team2Q1, team2Q2, team2Q3, team2Q4
Select league or season to track; Enter or import team roster and initial standings; Input game results as season progresses; View updated standings, upcoming schedule, and playoff bracket
compact alphanumeric code string (6-9 characters typical)

Create a bounded sovereign module for a grid-based puzzle level editor focused on Sokoban-like games. Key features: 1) A grid canvas for placing tiles on a mutable 2D grid, 2) A tile palette for selecting block/wall/goal/player types, 3) Level metadata (name, dimensions, author), 4) Text-based import/export with JSON format, 5) Format conversion between puzzle game formats. The module should be local-first, text-centric, and avoid generic asset management or broad game engine integration.

A parent or educator launches the module with a child profile (age 3-8), selects an activity zone, completes literacy tasks with real-time speech feedback, earns achievement badges, saves drawings to gallery, or initiates a two-player game with spoken prompts

Build a bounded squash referee scoreboard module: single-screen app showing current match score visible through court glass, serve-side indicator, point/let/conduct buttons, match-end detection for various scoring formats (PAR, handout, best-of-3/5, first-to-N), undo capability, and text-to-speech announcements of score in French and English. Must support singles and doubles serving rules.

Write a guided spelling adventure game where early literacy learners (grades 1-4) navigate a fantasy map, find hidden target words, and spell them correctly to progress, with character selection, hint system, and visual feedback.
region: Northern Africa, gameMode: timed, difficulty: standard, rounds: 10
